impact: This gives the PP&D daemon a deterministic recovery primitive for the current failure loop: after four consecutive failed or empty proposal rounds, the next prompt is constrained to one daemon-local fixture-first file set, complete JSON file replacements, and explicit bans on live DevHub sessions and broad contract rewrites. The file is intentionally small after repeated SyntaxError failures.
summary: Add a narrow, syntax-valid repeated no-accepted-patch guardrail helper with an embedded synthetic self-test.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 6 consecutive daemon rounds failed without an accepted patch

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
